turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: Hi there, thanks for seeing me. To be honest, I've been feeling really unwell for about three weeks now - my stomach has just been killing me and I've been having some pretty uncomfortable bowel movements.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: It's been really tough for me - I'm not sleeping well and it's affecting my daily life. I've tried to brush it off and think it'll go away on its own, but it's only getting worse.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: Yeah, besides the stomach pain and diarrhea, I've also been experiencing a low-grade fever a few times a week, it's not super high but it's just something that's always lingering in the back of my mind. And to be honest, I've also had some joint pain, achy knees and ankles.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: No, I don't usually get hot or sweaty, but sometimes I'll feel a bit lightheaded if I've had a bad episode and haven't eaten anything for a while.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The pain is mostly localized to my lower left abdomen, but sometimes it spreads or moves around.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've also been feeling a bit fatigued lately - it's affecting my work and I just don't have the energy to do things like I used to.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: No, no significant swelling that I've noticed.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: Actually, yeah... I get a bit queasy sometimes after eating, and I've had some nausea too. It's like my stomach is just really sensitive right now.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy levels have been pretty low lately - I feel tired all the time and it's affecting my work performance. And as for my appetite, I've actually noticed that food doesn't sit well with me anymore.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient:
I think I see what's going on here. You're trying to get all the facts out of me before we even start figuring out what's wrong with me, right? Well, let me tell you, I've been feeling really unwell for a while now - my stomach hurts, I'm having diarrhea, and I just feel tired all the time. And yeah, I have had some low-grade fevers, but like I said, they're not super high, just kind of lingering in the background.

As for night sweats, no, I don't think so... unless you count waking up from nocturnal diarrhea as a night sweat? That's been happening to me too, and it's really disrupting my sleep.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I had an appendectomy when I was 16 years old, but that was a long time ago and it didn't seem to cause any issues after that.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I've been trying to manage my symptoms on my own, but I'm not sure if that's the best approach. To be honest, I've noticed some changes in my body that are concerning me.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: I've been feeling pretty stressed lately and my eating habits have suffered as a result. I've been relying on takeout and convenience foods most of the time because they're quick and easy. Sometimes I'll grab something at a restaurant or order in, but it's not like I'm making healthy choices. To be honest, food hasn't been very enjoyable for me lately.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: Honestly, exercising has fallen by the wayside since my symptoms started. I've been trying to stay active in other ways, but lately, even just daily activities can be more tiring than they used to be.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30 years old.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: Yeah, I do worry about my own health, especially given how my symptoms are similar to hers.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I drink 2-3 beers most weeknights, but I have to admit that lately, I've increased it to 4-5 per night due to stress from my symptoms and work. As for coping with stress, I try to exercise or go for a walk when I can.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke or use any substances. Like I said, I do drink beer to cope with stress, but that's about it.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: I've been feeling really overwhelmed by all of this and I'm worried about what might be going on. It's getting to the point where I just can't ignore it anymore.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: I've been feeling really anxious about what could be causing all of this. The thought of not knowing what's going on is scary, and I'm worried about how it might affect my work and personal life.
